labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose

criticalHigh.action: review for causes of AKI, correct volume depletion, reassess concomitant medications

labs.actionRequired: reduce dose or consider dose reduction if concurrent volume depletion risk
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute kidney injury risk; diuretics can precipitate AKI

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose

criticalHigh.action: consider diuretics for hyperkalemia management when there is concomitant volume overload or hypertension

criticalLow.action: monitor for hypokalemia; response diminished with low eGFR

labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for electrolyte abnormalities; hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR; most compelling for hyperkalemia management when there is concomitant volume overload or hypertension; use with caution in patients with advanced CKD taking potassium-sparing diuretics
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Monitor serum creatinine and potassium within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose... Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities, and the hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR
